title: Share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus
caption: Between 2000 and 2022, the share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus through maternal immunization generally increased across six countries, though starting points and trajectories varied. Thailand, Mexico, and Brazil began the period with protection rates already above 80%, maintaining high levels throughout the timeframe and reaching near or at 100% by 2022. Vietnam and India started lower, around 80-85%, and showed steady upward trends, with Vietnam reaching approximately 95% and India approaching 90% by the end of the period. South Africa’s line begins below 70% in 2000, dips to just under 60% around 2005, then rises steadily to about 88% by 2022. All lines show non-declining trends after 2005, with most converging toward the upper end of the scale by 2022. The visible pattern shows sustained improvement over time, with all countries ending the period above 85% protection.
